mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that provides a robust platform for deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging for a more integrated experience. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management versus the powerful features and enterprise readiness it offers.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/tanzu

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Download the Tanzu CLI binary.
> Make the Tanzu CLI executable.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the necessary configurations.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> Initialize the Tanzu CLI with default settings.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified version and node count.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --plan dev --num-nodes 3

```

> Create a development cluster with 3 nodes.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --plan dev --num-nodes 3

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> Get the kubeconfig for admin access.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify that all nodes are in the Ready state.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the cluster and all associated resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> Delete the cluster and all its resources.

uninstall.title: Remove the Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> Delete the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Tanzu Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade the cluster version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup the current cluster state.
> Upgrade the cluster to the new version.
> Verify that all nodes are still in the Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified node count exceeds available resources.

**Fix:**
Reduce the number of nodes or increase available resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig context not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig context for the cluster is not set correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes returns no nodes'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not be fully initialized or is in a failed state.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status with 'tanzu cluster list' and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster upgrade failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The upgrade process encountered an issue, possibly due to incompatible configurations.

**Fix:**
Review the upgrade logs and ensure compatibility with the new version.

**Affected versions:** 2.4, 2.5

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.29, improved CLI performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure all custom resources are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in the Ready state.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --plan dev --num-nodes 3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
